channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: Baloch Rebels Claim They Killed Over 30 Pak Coast Guards In Suicide Bombing

media:content.media:description: The BLA is one of several separatist groups active in Balochistan. (Representational image)

item.description: The rebel group said the attack took place at approximately 6:32 pm on July 3, when a member of its elite Majeed Brigade allegedly drove an explosive-laden Mazda vehicle into the Coast Guards camp.
item.content:encoded: The rebel group said the attack took place at approximately 6:32 pm on July 3, when a member of its elite Majeed Brigade allegedly drove an explosive-laden Mazda vehicle into the Coast Guards camp.

item.title: "Send Them To Iran Or Cuba": Trump Spokeswoman Slams 'Lazy' Gen Z In Viral Remark

media:content.media:description: Karoline Leavitt is the 36th and current White House Press Secretary under President Donald Trump.

item.description: Leavitt's remarks quickly drew criticism online, with many commentators arguing that they overlooked the economic challenges facing young Americans.
item.content:encoded: Leavitt's remarks quickly drew criticism online, with many commentators arguing that they overlooked the economic challenges facing young Americans.

item.title: 'For Tibet': Tibetan Exile's Last Message As He Set Self On Fire Outside UN Office

media:content.media:description: News of his death sent shockwaves through the Tibetan people.

item.description: The self-immolation comes at a symbolically charged time for the exile community, with preparations underway for the Dalai Lama's upcoming 91st birthday celebrations.
item.content:encoded: The self-immolation comes at a symbolically charged time for the exile community, with preparations underway for the Dalai Lama's upcoming 91st birthday celebrations.

item.title: Pakistan Deputy PM Ishaq Dar's Relative Arrested In Gangrape Of Foreign Women

media:content.media:description: Raza Dar, identified as the prime suspect, is linked to senior politician Ishaq Dar

item.description: The suspects, identified as Muhammad Raza Dar, Hassan Raza, Sikandar Khan and Sajid Ali, were sent to five-day police remand by a Lahore court on Friday.
item.content:encoded: The suspects, identified as Muhammad Raza Dar, Hassan Raza, Sikandar Khan and Sajid Ali, were sent to five-day police remand by a Lahore court on Friday.

item.title: "Usually Read Stories About Myself": Trump On Usha Vance's Podcast

media:content.media:description: Trump pretaped the appearance in mid-June in the White House's Oval Office.

item.description: When Vance asked Trump if he has much time to read for fun while serving as president, he replied that he ends up mostly reading newspapers.
item.content:encoded: When Vance asked Trump if he has much time to read for fun while serving as president, he replied that he ends up mostly reading newspapers.

item.title: 'I Should Invite Obama, Biden': Trump Floats White House Football Reunion

media:content.media:description: Trump has left no opportunity to criticise Obama and mock Biden (File)

item.description: Donald Trump made the suggestion on the podcast hosted by US Second Lady Usha Vance which was released on Friday ahead of the 250th anniversary of American Independence.
item.content:encoded: Donald Trump made the suggestion on the podcast hosted by US Second Lady Usha Vance which was released on Friday ahead of the 250th anniversary of American Independence.

item.title: Pegasus Strikes Again: Politician Investigating Spyware Abuse Ends Up Hacked

media:content.media:description: This reignites controversy over governments using spyware to target critics illegally.

item.description: Security researchers have confirmed that a European politician had his phone hacked with the Pegasus spyware while serving on an investigatory committee probing abuses of the notorious surveillance tool.
item.content:encoded: Security researchers have confirmed that a European politician had his phone hacked with the Pegasus spyware while serving on an investigatory committee probing abuses of the notorious surveillance tool.

item.title: Iranian Speaker, Foreign Minister Break Down At Ali Khamenei's Farewell

media:content.media:description: Bagher Ghalibaf and Abbas Araghchi broke down in tears during the farewell ceremony Ayatollah Khamenei.

item.description: Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi broke down in tears during the farewell ceremony for former Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in Tehran on Friday.
item.content:encoded: <iframe title="Player" class="head-story__img img__full lazyload" height="300" scrolling=no frameborder=no src="about:blank" data-src="https://www.ndtv.com/video/embed-player/id/1122364/play-via-app-wap?pWidth=100&pHeight=100&embed_type=story&site=classic&autostart=1"> </iframe>Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi broke down in tears during the farewell ceremony for former Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in Tehran on Friday.

item.title: "Mind Your...": Iran Hits Back At Trump Over "Hungry Nation" Remarks

media:content.media:description: Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf on Thursday criticised Donald Trump over his remarks on Iran's economy.

item.description: Iran on Thursday criticised US President Donald Trump over his remarks on Iran's economy and food needs, accusing him of projecting America's own domestic challenges after Trump said the United States hoped to export food commodities to Iran.
item.content:encoded: <iframe title="Player" class="head-story__img img__full lazyload" height="300" scrolling=no frameborder=no src="about:blank" data-src="https://www.ndtv.com/video/embed-player/id/1122355/play-via-app-wap?pWidth=100&pHeight=100&embed_type=story&site=classic&autostart=1"> </iframe>Iran on Thursday criticised US President Donald Trump over his remarks on Iran's economy and food needs, accusing him of projecting America's own domestic challenges after Trump said the United States hoped to export food commodities to Iran.

item.title: 5.5 Magnitude Earthquake Hits Chile
item.description: An earthquake measuring 5.5 magnitude struck near coast of central Chile on Friday, the German Research Centre for Geosciences (GFZ) said.
item.content:encoded: An earthquake measuring 5.5 magnitude struck near coast of central Chile on Friday, the German Research Centre for Geosciences (GFZ) said.

item.title: Brazil Court Rules Jair Bolsonaro To Remain Under House Arrest
item.description: Brazil's Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes has ruled that former Brazilian President Jair Bolsonaro can remain under house arrest due to health concerns, a court decision showed on Friday.
item.content:encoded: Brazil's Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes has ruled that former Brazilian President Jair Bolsonaro can remain under house arrest due to health concerns, a court decision showed on Friday.

item.title: Russia Says It Seized Eastern Ukrainian Stronghold Of Kostyantynivka

media:content.media:description: Russia said that Ukraine's Kostyantynivka has been its under control.

item.description: Russia on Friday said it had captured the eastern Ukrainian city of Kostyantynivka, a key post on the road leading to the last major cities in the Donbas region under Kyiv's control.
item.content:encoded: Russia on Friday said it had captured the eastern Ukrainian city of Kostyantynivka, a key post on the road leading to the last major cities in the Donbas region under Kyiv's control.

item.title: Venezuela Earthquake: Death Count Rises To 2,645, Over 12,600 Injured

media:content.media:description: Thousands more are reported still missing after the June 24 back-to-back quakes.

item.description: The death toll from Venezuela's devastating twin earthquakes has risen to 2,645, with more than 12,600 injured, according to new officia figures released on Friday.
item.content:encoded: The death toll from Venezuela's devastating twin earthquakes has risen to 2,645, with more than 12,600 injured, according to new officia figures released on Friday.

item.title: Heatwave In US Delays 250th Independence Day Celebrations

media:content.media:description: New York City's heat index reached 106 degrees on Thursday.

item.description: A blistering heat wave gripping several states across the East Coast of the US prompted the closure of the Great American State Fair here Friday and forced cancellations of the Independence Day celebrations across the region.
item.content:encoded: A blistering heat wave gripping several states across the East Coast of the US prompted the closure of the Great American State Fair here Friday and forced cancellations of the Independence Day celebrations across the region.

item.title: Deaths Surged 29% In France During Week Of Record Heat, Health Agency Says

media:content.media:description: In Paris, funeral service directors have said they've struggled to find places to store bodies

item.description: The new and still incomplete figures from Public Health France doubled its first preliminary estimate of at least 1,000 additional deaths that it gave last Sunday. That earlier estimate covered just three of the hottest days of extreme, deadly heat.
item.content:encoded: The new and still incomplete figures from Public Health France doubled its first preliminary estimate of at least 1,000 additional deaths that it gave last Sunday. That earlier estimate covered just three of the hottest days of extreme, deadly heat.

item.title: On Usha Vance's "Book-Reading" Podcast, Trump Discusses Past Presidents

media:content.media:description: Trump mused about riding a horse after seeing a picture of Abraham Lincoln riding one.

item.description: As Trump turned through the children's book, he made observations about past presidents, cracked some jokes, and threw in a plug for the extensive ballroom he's building on the White House grounds.
item.content:encoded: As Trump turned through the children's book, he made observations about past presidents, cracked some jokes, and threw in a plug for the extensive ballroom he's building on the White House grounds.

item.title: Trump And Netanyahu Hold Phone Call, Agree To Meet "Soon"

media:content.media:description: Trump has been publicly critical of Netanyahu in recent weeks. (File)

item.description: The conversation comes after the United States and Israel fought alongside each other in their military campaign against Iran.
item.content:encoded: The conversation comes after the United States and Israel fought alongside each other in their military campaign against Iran.

item.title: Trump Tightened H-1B Visa Rules, Then Renewals Hit Record High

media:content.media:description: The rise comes as Trump administration attempts to tighten parts of the H-1B system

item.description: The government had proposed a $100,000 fee on many new H-1B applications filed for workers outside the US, but a federal judge blocked the move in June.
item.content:encoded: The government had proposed a $100,000 fee on many new H-1B applications filed for workers outside the US, but a federal judge blocked the move in June.

item.title: Russia Jails 12 'Neo-Nazi' Group Members For Planning To Kill Media Mogul

media:content.media:description: Russian prosecutors did not give an exact motive for the plot.

item.description: Among the other crimes of which the 12 were accused were attacks on migrants and LGBTQ people, as well as committing robberies and inciting ethnic hatred.
item.content:encoded: Among the other crimes of which the 12 were accused were attacks on migrants and LGBTQ people, as well as committing robberies and inciting ethnic hatred.

item.title: Putin Says Fuel Shortage "Not Critical" As Russia Ramps Up Attack On Ukraine

media:content.media:description: Putin has described the attacks on Russian energy as an effort by Ukraine to distract attention.

item.description: The Russian leader appears to believe his government can keep the fuel crisis from eroding his authority and support for the war he launched
item.content:encoded: The Russian leader appears to believe his government can keep the fuel crisis from eroding his authority and support for the war he launched
